reason: Load-bearing dependency on node 1.3 still undeclared (round-2 ch-e76396b85e85de50 only PARTIALLY resolved). The node's first and load-bearing step is the opening identity P-Phi=(I+SR)/2-(I+S)/2=S(R-I)/2, which substitutes the value P=(I+SR)/2. The ENTIRE node (delta=||P-Phi|| and ||P||<=1+C eta) rests on this value of P. But P=(I+SR)/2 is established NOWHERE in node 1.5 and NOWHERE in any of the four nodes it names (1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2): I grep-checked all four and none defines P, sgn(S), theta, or the identity P=(I+SR)/2. The value P=(I+sgn(S))/2 is proven SOLELY in node 1.3 (which sets P=theta(2Phi-I)=(I+sgn(S))/2 with sgn(S)=S(S^2)^(-1/2)), and sgn(S)=SR uses R=(S^2)^(-1/2) from node 1.2. Thus the opening identity genuinely depends on node 1.3 (composed with 1.2). Round-2 challenge ch-e76396b85e85de50 explicitly demanded 'Declare 1.3 (and 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2) as dependencies.' The revision added 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2 to the in-text 'Uses nodes' line but OMITTED 1.3 -- the one node carrying the load-bearing opening identity. Per the in-text-dependency rule for this round, the node's stated dependency set is incorrect/incomplete: it must name node 1.3 (for P=(I+sgn(S))/2 = (I+SR)/2) as the source of the opening identity. As written, the very first equation is unsupported by the node's own declared dependencies.
